sections.paragraph:
συνταράσσω fut. συνταράξω LXX; 1 aor. συνετάραξα. Pass. 1 fut. 3 pl. συνταραχθήσονται (En 99:4); 1 aor. συνεταράχθν LXX (Hom. [in tmesis] et al.) to throw into confusion, disturb (Hdt., Thu. et al.; Pla., Leg. 7, 798A συνταραχθεὶς ὑπὸ νόσων; OGI 669, 41 [I a.d.]; PGM 36, 142; LXX; En 99:4; TestJud 14:3; Just., D. 124, 1) Lk 9:42 D.

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pl. = plural

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 974
